description: in dev mode, make sure we are updating current_room value apprpriatly. when we open the room config pannel make sure we are loading the correct room (the current room) make sure that we can only select current rooms assets in room editor (but also boundary and map wide assets when in that selection mode )

depth effects textures need to be applied to the dynamically spawned assets. currently they are not. same method of calculation and rendering for the foreground background textures on the dynamically spawned assets
in normal game mode the transition from camera settings in one room to another room needs to be way more smooth

Trail generation performance is slow and produces uniform-width trails. In ENGINE/runtime/gameplay/map_generation/ find the trail generation logic and optimize it. Instead of uniform width implement variable width trails by adding min_width and max_width parameters to trail room configuration so trails appear more organic and natural. The trail type is identified by room->type == "trail" (checked via vibble::strings::to_lower_copy). Width variation should be applied during trail mesh/area generation. Key files: ENGINE/runtime/gameplay/map_generation/room.hpp (Room class trail detection), ENGINE/runtime/gameplay/map_generation/ (trail/room generation code).

In ENGINE/runtime/animation/get_best_path.cpp and ENGINE/runtime/animation/movement_plan_executor.cpp add collision overlap prevention logic to the auto-move and pathfinding system for NPCs and enemies. When computing movement paths check for potential overlaps with other NPCs/enemies in the target area and either choose an alternate path or adjust movement to prevent stacking/clipping. The current GetBestPath() and MovementPlanExecutor classes do not account for dynamic NPC positions during path planning. Key files: ENGINE/runtime/animation/get_best_path.cpp (GetBestPath function), ENGINE/runtime/animation/movement_plan_executor.cpp (MovementPlanExecutor class), ENGINE/runtime/gameplay/world/ (world entity management).

Add a Create Room button to the dev mode footer in ENGINE/editor/devtools/dev_controls.cpp. For the Layers panel in dev mode replace the current layers UI with the same RoomConfigurator side panel (ENGINE/editor/devtools/config/room_config/room_configurator.cpp) used in room editor mode. This provides a consistent room editing experience between dev mode and room editor. The RoomConfigurator class already handles room geometry camera spawn groups and tags - integrate it into the dev mode layers panel. Key files: ENGINE/editor/devtools/dev_controls.cpp (dev mode UI DevControls class), ENGINE/editor/devtools/config/room_config/room_configurator.cpp (RoomConfigurator class).

In ENGINE/runtime/assets/asset/Asset.hpp and ENGINE/runtime/utils/utils/AnchorPointResolver.cpp fix child asset anchor point placement with movement data. Child assets should first be placed at their parent's anchor point (current behavior) then if the child asset has movement data defined in its AnimationFrame::movement array apply the dx dy dz movement offsets relative to the calculated anchor point position. The final spawn position = anchor_point_world_pos + movement_delta. This ensures child assets with animation movement correctly offset from their parent anchor spawn location while maintaining the existing child asset spawning pipeline. Key files: ENGINE/runtime/assets/asset/Asset.hpp (Asset class child asset handling), ENGINE/runtime/utils/utils/AnchorPointResolver.cpp (anchor_points::resolve_anchor_point()), ENGINE/runtime/assets/asset/anchor_point.hpp (DisplacedAssetAnchorPoint struct).
